title: Querying Data: SELECT
description: Retrieve data using query() and fetch().

slides.title: PDO::query()
slides.content:
# Simple SELECT with query()

If your query has no user input, you can use `query()` to execute it directly. It returns a `PDOStatement` object.

```php
$stmt = $pdo->query('SELECT * FROM users');
```

Then you can fetch rows with `fetch()` or `fetchAll()`.

slides.title: Fetching Data
slides.codeSnippet:
<?php
$stmt = $pdo->query('SELECT id, name, email FROM users');
while ($row = $stmt->fetch()) {
    echo $row['name'] . ' - ' . $row['email'] . "\n";
}
?>

quizData.question: What does PDO::query() return?
quizData.explanation: query() returns a PDOStatement object that you can use to fetch data.
